overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from GTA Vice City Definitive Edition showing the protagonist Tommy Vercetti walking along the Ocean Drive-inspired waterfront promenade. The character passes colorful art deco hotels with neon accents, palm trees, and pedestrians in 1980s beachwear while the in-game HUD displays health, armor, and mini-map.

scene_state.functional_zone: oceanfront hotel promenade with art deco architecture

agents.ego_description: walking steadily along the sidewalk past colorful beachfront hotels and neon-lit buildings

notable_agents.action: pedestrian in yellow shorts roller skating past the character
pedestrian in orange swimsuit walking on the grassy area

entered.detail: art deco building with prominent vertical neon signage spelling DAKOTA
row of colorful 1980s style hotels with purple and green neon accents
tall palm trees lining the grassy area between sidewalk and beach
well-maintained green lawn running parallel to the sidewalk

segments.scene_description: The segment opens with the character walking along a vibrant oceanfront promenade. The Dakota building with its vertical neon signage dominates the initial view, while colorful art deco hotels with neon purple and green accents line the left side. Palm trees and a grassy lawn separate the sidewalk from the beach area to the right, and pedestrians in 1980s beachwear stroll along the path.

events.description: on-screen text 'Washington Beach' appears in bottom right corner identifying the location

agents.ego_description: continuing along the palm-lined promenade with increasing pedestrian traffic

notable_agents.action: pedestrian in blue bikini walking alongside the character on the grass
pedestrian in red outfit walking along the sidewalk in the distance

entered.detail: tall modern building visible at the end of the hotel strip

segments.scene_description: The character continues walking along the open waterfront promenade as the Dakota building recedes behind. The art deco hotels remain prominent on the left while dense palm trees line the grassy right side. More pedestrians in beachwear appear, and vehicle traffic increases slightly on the multi-lane road. The location is identified as Washington Beach by on-screen text.

scene_state.functional_zone: street crossing and opposite sidewalk near Moonlite Hotel

events.description: character crosses the multi-lane street from the sidewalk to the opposite side
character turns around to face back toward the hotel strip while continuing to walk

agents.ego_description: crossing the street and turning to view the art deco buildings from the opposite sidewalk

notable_agents.action: pedestrian in white tank top crossing the street close to the character
blue sedan driving down the street past the crossing character

entered.detail: multi-story art deco hotel with illuminated 'Moonlite Hotel' signage
zebra crossing lines painted on the asphalt road surface

segments.scene_description: The character leaves the grassy promenade area and crosses the multi-lane street, navigating between passing vehicles. Upon reaching the opposite side, the Moonlite Hotel with its distinctive signage becomes visible. The character turns around, shifting the perspective to show the art deco hotel strip from the opposite side of the road as the video concludes.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement along a vibrant oceanfront promenade that eventually shifts to a perspective from across a busy city street. It begins with the character walking past the Dakota building and a row of art deco hotels accented with purple and green neon, while pedestrians in 1980s beachwear stroll beside a grassy lawn and palm trees. As the Dakota building recedes, the camera continues forward through the Washington Beach area, where the density of palm trees and vehicle traffic on the adjacent multi-lane road increases. The scene then shifts as the character leaves the promenade to cross the street between passing cars, bringing the distinctive signage of the Moonlite Hotel into view. Finally, the camera turns around to reveal the entire art deco hotel strip from the opposite side of the road.
